title: Ruins of Mairaath
original_text:
Nightmarish storms circled the globe, stirring the ashes of a murdered world.

Even after the apocalyptic ruin of Mairaath humanity still huddled in the broken shells of great cities brought low, blasted by unending wind.

title: Eochu Bres, Xanadu
original_text: Shining Tri-Tachyon Corporation arcologies towered over a riotous sprawl of shantytown conurbation. Company salariat were born, lived, and died protected by security drones in the high city Xanadu while a desperate class of immiserated laborers dwelled in the low city where anything at all was bought or sold.

title: Eochu Bres, Skyarco II
original_text:
Gleaming monoliths glittered, spears of capital upthrust through the glowering flesh of the world. Skyarco II loomed prismatic.

The shuttle alit on a landing pad held at some distance from the facade by an airy support rib. It looked impossibly thin, but after touch down felt solid as a mountain.

title: Planetary Survey
original_text:
The fringe of the Persean Second is filled with worlds lost, forgotten, and never seen at all by consciousness born of flesh.

There are green fields of toxic xenolife, tombs frosted with nitrogen snow, roiling cauldrons of magma and brimstone, and planets made of living storm; vast fortunes to be uncovered, barbarian-worlds incapable of starflight, and the weapons of ancient wars primed to attack any interloper.

title: Luddic Shrine
original_text:
Pilgrims were offered tea, water, and ritual washing. The air was filled with sweat and incense. All around, a river of faith and ritual flowed.
In the hardship of life in the Persean Sector, as in the hardship of the pilgrimage, the Faithful found a kind of peace and meaning in the vacuum left by the Collapse.

title: Orbital Construction
original_text: The ceramoid plating slowly peeled away in an aeon-long cascading fail state. Metallic ribs exposed, bleached by stellar winds, charged and released the pulsing dance of magnetic fields, the thrumming of orbits and the gnawing sirocco of micrometeorites.

title: Dead Gate
original_text: "Humanity was thence scattered abroad upon the face of the galaxy, speaking one tongue, united by the demon Gates in one body of worldly law. But we had forgotten the laws of God, mightier than any fusion lance! God would remind humanity of its place in this universe."

title: Active Gate
original_text:
It is a humming ring of adamantine material. If one knows how to look, sensors can detect their faint vibration in higher dimensions.

It is like a barely heard song, a door waiting to be opened.

title: Eventide
original_text: Leading families of Eventide sponsored the stellar mirrors and shades to maintain islands of comfortable habitability. The disgraced and poor, meanwhile, lived in the twilight peripheries where harsh climactic shifts of the slow summer-day/winter-night cycle had to be endured in full force.

title: Volturn, Ocean World
original_text: The port of Volturn was built on a loose flotilla of huge, singed-looking barges that wallowed in an oily sea. Offloading spacers are interrogated by dour security officers, then allowed into the traders' concourse. Merchants in dull suits, loyalty pins dutifully displayed on the collar, passed around a datapad loaded with incoming cargo manifests.
